tagline: Rye and absinthe, sealed with wax.
instructions: Rinse a chilled rocks glass with the absinthe; discard the excess.
In a mixing glass, combine rye, syrup, and bitters with ice.
Stir for 30 rotations.
Strain into the prepared glass. No ice in the serving glass.
omen: Anise ghosts the rim and the rye fills the room it vacated. This drink was invented before most of your certainties, and it will outlast the rest. Sip it as a closing argument, not an opening statement.

tagline: A vow of smoke and bitter prayer.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir for 25 rotations.
Strain into a rocks glass over one large hand-cut cube.
Express an orange peel and rest it on the rim.
omen: The Pechuga was distilled with fruit and a whole chicken breast, which is something you now know and cannot unknow. The bitterness is Campari; the silence is everything else. Drink this alone. You will be better company afterward.

tagline: Written in ink that only appears by firelight.
instructions: Combine whisky, charcoal syrup, and bitters in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir for 30 rotations.
Strain into a rocks glass over a hand-carved ice sphere.
Finish with a pinch of smoked salt over the top.
garnish: expressed orange peel, smoked salt rim (half)
omen: Black as a judge's notebook and twice as revealing. The charcoal strips everything back to the whisky's skeleton, and the skeleton is more beautiful than the body ever was. You will look at something familiar and see its architecture for the first time.

tagline: A toast to everyone who should not be here but is.
instructions: Place the sugar cube in a chilled flute.
Saturate it with bitters.
Pour the cognac over the sugar.
Top slowly with cold Champagne.
omen: The sugar cube dissolves and the bitters climb through the Champagne like a rumor through a crowded room. You will raise this glass for someone who is not present, and they will know, somehow, that you did.

tagline: Peace terms written on edible gold.
instructions: Pour the Chartreuse into a large wine glass with ice.
Top with Champagne and soda.
Stir once, gently.
Lay the gold leaf across the surface with tweezers.
omen: Gold floats on gold. The Chartreuse VEP is older than most marriages and more carefully guarded, and the Champagne defers to it the way water defers to stone. You will feel, briefly, like someone whose name is remembered in rooms they have left.

instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with hand-cut ice.
Stir slowly — 40 rotations — until velvet-cold.
Strain into a chilled coupe.
Garnish with a Luxardo cherry on a pick.
omen: The rye is ten years in the asking and one sip in the answering. Oak and spice and the quiet understanding that some gifts arrive with invoices attached. Read the fine print; it is worth the price.

tagline: Left to you in a locked cabinet, with instructions.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir for 30 rotations until silky.
Strain into a chilled Nick and Nora glass.
Express a lemon peel over the surface and discard.
omen: Older than the Martini and less interested in your opinion of it. The Old Tom gin is sweetened, the vermouth is serious, and the maraschino is the clause in the will that nobody expected. You are inheriting something tonight. Drink carefully.

tagline: The last entry in a ledger no one else can read.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice.
Shake until extremely cold.
Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
Serve without garnish.
omen: The VEP has aged in oak for longer than most New Year's resolutions survive, and it shows. Herbal, insistent, and entirely sure of itself. You will close a chapter tonight, and the closing will feel like opening a window.

instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice.
Shake gently — 8 seconds, no more. This drink does not like to be rushed.
Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
Float a single rose petal on the surface.
omen: Rose and lychee over Japanese oak, and the combination should not work but it does, the way some people should not work but do. You will touch someone's hand tonight, or you will wish you had. The whisky will not tell you which.

tagline: Purple ink on vellum, still wet.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice.
Shake until very cold.
Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
Express a lime peel over the surface.
omen: Violet and agave in a frequency only certain ears can hear. The tequila is 30 months in the barrel and the Creme de Violette has not changed its recipe since 1845. Between them they know more about patience than you do. Listen.

tagline: No rebuttal. No appeal. No ice.
instructions: Rinse a rocks glass with absinthe; discard the excess.
Combine remaining ingredients in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir for 30 rotations.
Strain into the prepared glass over one large hand-cut cube.
omen: Pre-Prohibition and unimpressed by everything that followed. The absinthe is a ghost in the room; the bourbon is the room. You will make a decision tonight that is ten years overdue, and the drink will not congratulate you, but it will nod.

tagline: A fortress that drinks like a fire.

ingredients.name: Thomas H. Handy Sazerac rye (barrel proof)

instructions: Rinse a chilled rocks glass with absinthe; discard the excess.
In a mixing glass, muddle the syrup and bitters.
Add the rye and ice; stir for 25 rotations.
Strain into the prepared glass. No ice.
omen: Barrel-proof rye at the gate and absinthe holding the walls. This drink does not negotiate, and tonight neither should you. Whatever you have been circling, walk toward it. The rye has been waiting longer than you have.

tagline: Vespers ring. The day is finally done.
instructions: Warm a heatproof glass with hot water; discard.
Add rum, Benedictine, lemon, and honey syrup.
Top with hot water and stir gently.
Garnish and drink with both hands.
omen: The rum is twenty years old and has nothing left to prove. The Benedictine hums a hymn you half-remember, and the honey agrees. You have earned this, though not in the way you think. Rest now. The bell tolls for no one tonight.

tagline: Prescribed for winters of the soul.
instructions: Prepare rich hot chocolate with whole milk and dark chocolate.
Pour the cognac into a warmed mug.
Add the hot chocolate and vanilla.
Stir gently and finish with a pinch of fleur de sel.
omen: Chocolate and brandy and the particular mercy of salt on something sweet. This drink does not ask you to be brave or clever or even awake for much longer. It asks only that you be warm, and that is enough.
